Both the US and Europe are in decline, but both have very far to fall, in spite of acceleration. The US has always outperformed Europe economically, while Europe has mogged the US in history, cultural diversity, and art.

62388366
>>62388362
And science and tech. The pipeline is often that europe invents some pivotal tech but fails to monetize it because the infrastructure for this is dogshit in europe, so either some us company copies it and wins the market, the european company moves to the us, or a us company purchases the european company and markets the tech through the main office.

62388374
>>62388366
I agree but it's also the pipeline of the past. I'd also add that the US university research system is pretty good at science and tech discovery as well. But lately, a lot of the monetization and scaling manufacturing for non software tech discovered or invented in the US or Europe has come from China.

62388389
>>62388374
Yes, it's true that modern US research unis are pretty good. However they are much better at publicizing their work than they are in actually doing novel work, in relative terms, compared to their EU peers. For example, pretty much all modern biology works on purely EU tech. But you will only read about what the US has contributed (or thinks it might contribute some day) if you look up information about biology advances.

But this has largely equalized for salaries (not quite there yet but you could say it's there if you add the work conditions and life conditions in the US have greatly deteriorated and many are no longer willing to get the salary difference to put up with it). It hasn't yet equalized for funding though, but it's become just hard enough to raise a business instead of a scam in the US that people who want to actually make real businesses don't feel the need to go to the US anymore (more like, US investors no longer invite them in, as it were).

Most of the prescriptions were for xanax and such, and he would ring them up himself and then sell them to pill junkies. I only found out about it when I filled in another few stores and saw the same thing there. I asked him about it and he denied it, but I knew and didn't tell anything to anyone.
Anyway he did this for a while and when there was the 10k bitcoin for pizza came out, I remember him being super bullish about it saying he worked 'extra' to buy as many bitcoin as possible. He would even take diabetic test strips and just walk out with them...outright stealing. He would show me them and say '2 boxes equals 1 bitcoin' and laugh at me. I was like wagging my finger and saying 'you'll learn'. Fast forward to 2017 and I ran into him at a gas station literally driving a Lambo. He said I should have listened to him and the last thing he said was 'Crime pays', and that was the last I heard from him.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 120 replies — 38.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 120 • Updated: 2026-06-22 15:47 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62392261
>>62392213
Another thing is that we want to leverage the amount of anons who will frequent these threads, and likely spend a limited time (emphasis on limited) and will only be able to make 4-5 posts before heading out.

Compare that to the amount of /ic/ anons you want to gather? A number of 10-15 draw anons is poor compared to the 50-60 unique anons who might post more on our topics of study and likely can push our plan further along

Drawing takes time and a lot of energy. We're just trying to get above $10k. If we had these /ic/ anons in here at best we'd have 4-5(AT BEST) working and 50-60 anons only able to really focus on 2/3 of the topics because one topic is gate kept for drawfags

Anon. If you want to make your own threads and own path, then go for it. I can't reasonably see it doing well here.

Finally, is the time. We've got 90 days before we switch topics. That time factor is important. We have to adapt. We can't waste a year dedicated to shrimp farming. 90 days. We have to move fast, the great reset is upon us. Drawing is antithetical to that 90 day margin.

I said what I mainly want to say. Don't feel the need to be polite. If you feel passionate about this and have to respond like a chansman then do that. I believe your plan could be done, just perhaps in another space. Not mine.

Thank you for showing me Gabriel Tenma

62392264
>>62392239
You could have picked anything other than drawing, even coding or vibe coding shit. Drawing has a horrible ROI unless you are a savant.

62392267
>>62392261
One of our topics has been chosen.

Let us take a moment to soak this in before I discuss the second. I am quite skilled in it, and I believe many of you are as well. I alluded to it briefly earlier.

62392272
>>62392264
Yes, that is a point I wanted to emphasize as well

62392301
Drawing does take a lot of time and it is true that the ROI is practically non-existent which explains the endless seethe coming from /ic/ kek. The exceptional ones are those who managed to fool enough people into subscribing to their blogs through a paywall, they shill themselves very much like a shitcoin. That's what I'm trying to put together, art and the whole shitcoin element that involves the concept of "working for your bags" for an ROI after tokenizing a narrative, a meme, and in this case a hobby.

Pic related is a good example, someone liked this enough to steal it and started to use this drawing to shill their own shitcoin with a couple years ago.

62392302
>>62385259

Starfall demo is on Tuesday, and Starship flight 13 may fly as early as July.

62392310
>>62392301
and by stealing I mean that in a good way since that's what we do anyway we go around stealing content from others and recreate it into something of our own.

62392312
I know I'm technically not part of this club but I'm desperate for answers anyways.
> 34
> $30k net worth
That net worth was once $150k but crypto absolutely bludgeoned me and wiped me out + living expenses ate up a solid $30k-$50k of that over the past several years.

Desperate for answers because I've been unemployed for a few years, yeah CS meme degree if any of you are wondering. I've applied to around 400 jobs since late last year but only a few measly phone screens and no actual interviews yet. Endless rejections and I'm at the end of my rope. What type of jobs will allow me to career pivot? It seems impossible to pivot these days when every hiring manager wants 2-5 years of (proven) niche experience. That and fuck ass HR uses AI to screen candidates at literally every company these days, it's fucking impossible to get noticed in front of literally hundreds of applicants per job.

62392373
>>62392301
>>62392310
You'll have to do remain in the background anon, do what you must with this real estate, I'm gonna move on with the main 3

62393422
The 2nd topic we shall devote our time to studying is the art of predictive programming.

>Gematria, movies, astrology etc

Whatever helps us understand how to predict future events. I don't see why we couldn't have predicted Hantavirus, Trump's failed assassination attempt or the death of Oliver Tree.

It's time we stop showing up late to the party and screaming THEY CAN'T KEEP GETTING AWAY WITH THIS

It's time we show up early. If we can get some scripted events predicted early, we could make a bet on polymarket/kalshi or just launch a shitcoin for PENNIES and then sit back as everyone apes in.

I know this sounds farfetched but we have to believe in ourselves. If THEY can do it, so can WE.

In 2019, The most popular song was Old Town Road, Lil Nas X and Billy Ray Cyrus

Lil = Wind, Breeze, Air or Phantom (sumerian)
Nas = ???
X = death

Billy = ???
Ray = light, solar
Cyrus = Virus, Cyrus the great was said to be named after the sun

The next remix(in January 2020) had Diplo, BTS and Mason Ramsey

Mason Ramsey = this is obvious
BTS = Asia
Diplo = ???

Obvious this wouldn't be enough to predict covid-19 however it's a start. We would've known that Old Town Road was insanely popular during 2019 and we would've just had to figure out what it was alluding to. With all of us giving a shot, we might've been able to short the market.

This will be our second topic, Predictive Programming

62393434
>>62385679
Not a bad idea, but can we trust at least 30% of the anons here to have a laptop/PC that can run Godot?

62393449
I am still thinking of the third Topic we should spend our time learning. I will figure it out today. I chose one that I liked (predictive programming) and the photonics anons suggestion I thought was great and realistic. The third one I'd like for the thread to suggest but most suggestions here are "DCA" and "get a job"

Feel free to suggest something. Even if this is the only time you'll be here. I'd like for us to have the third Topic by today

62393459
>>62392312
Download the app Blind. Filled with guys trying to get into FAGMAN. Might find a crumb there.

62393620
Okay guys as we wait for the third topic, let’s go ahead and get started

First off, why should we care about photonics at this point in time? Well while the field itself might sound like sci-fi to the layman (insert schizo theories on light healing, lightsabers or consciousness transfer) we want to focus on the more grounded reality of what we’re dealing with today. AI.

We all know the Jews have massive amounts of data. They have it. How they collected is not relevant nor is the why. They have it. The main reason everyone is talking about photonics right now is because they are struggling to move the data from point A to B

Copper is what has generally handled this role, but they consume significant power, generate heat, and become less efficient at the massive scales Jews are targeting. Copper probably won’t just completely exit the industry however there will most certainly be a reduction.

62395577
Brainstorming

Software created current-state AI, AI created current state demand for hardware resources, hardware surge created demand for production materials, hardware costs contribute to higher cost datacentre build costs, running the datacentres with current state AI running on current state hardware consumes higher energy demand.

>AI - priced in
>Hardware - priced in
>Materials - priced in
>Datacentres - priced in
>Energy - priced in

So the disruptor to this ecosystem might be sitting where we started - software development. The race to highly energy-efficient AI models is the next logical thing. The current trajectory is unsustainable from a resources perspective, and the financial burn rate is unsustainable.

You make AI consumption highly resource efficient, all of those things come down as the demand reduces. Or does supply just increase to fill the increased capacity?

That takes me to the next innovative era that will consume the increased capacity;
> Robotics and humanoids / androids - building interest but probably not fully priced in yet
> Transport and automated movement - AI powered, driverless vehicles (short Uber)
> Highly efficient reusable rocketry - transport anything anywhere quickly (reusability powered by AI robotics)
> Personal digital self's - Consumer facing AI-version-of-you running your life, in your house, ordering your groceries - your life on autopilot (think a personal JARVIS)
> medical application - localised AI models in hospitals and medical rooms - control robotics in surgery
> mobile devices - uncertain future, at some point the personal handheld device model may come to an end

So my take away for a macro, strategic tech play could be to accumulate software and tech players with resources that can produce highly efficient AI models that use less resources - Microsoft is trading at a discount? - instead of over-committing to hardware and the fundamentals. Model efficiency could improve at any stage.

Thoughts?

>>rent is significantly higher than mortgae payment for a 3 ned / 2 bath home with yard and 2 car garage
This means that you live in an economically depressed area. Nobody is buying those homes even though the mortgage is cheaper because all of the locals are too broke to qualify for a mortgage under current lending standards.
>but why won't landlords buy it?
Landlords only want to buy at a price where the mortgage is below the market rental rate, because they have other fixed costs before getting their margin (maintenance insurance etc).
Note that if you lived somewhere in high demand, like LA, landlords would buy anyways and eat the short-term losses to try and ride the wave of price appreciation. But if you live in a shithole that's liable to Detroitify itself at any moment, then they won't fall for that.
>How is the 1 cuckroom apartment more appealing to people?
It's not. That's just the most that the system we live under trusts them to have.

Further reading: https://kevinerdmann.substack.com/p/what-kalamazoo-reveals-about-the

62397036
>>62395241
The reason usually has to do with total cost. The break-even point for home ownership being worth the cost is typically around 5-7 years. If you are planning on staying somewhere 7-10 years, you may come out financially ahead. But when you factor in down payments, closing costs, maintenance, property taxes, and insurance, renters tend to be better off in the short term. Renting is usually the lower risk option.

If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

LINK is a $5.7B token on $57.5M of annual fees. That’s ~99x revenue, and it has been flat for years, straight through every “banks are coming” cycle. The promise was always that adoption converts into value. It hasn’t. It’s been “soon” since 2020.

So here’s the question no diehard wants to answer:

What fee number, by what date, would make you sell? If LINK still earns ~$57M in 2028, is the thesis wrong, or is it still “soon”? If you can’t name the number and the date, you’re not investing in Chainlink. You’re believing in it. And belief has no exit.

Anon is relaxing

If anything happens to me that seem to be a good thing, I have 6 months max to enjoy it because that'll be taken from me and then I'll be beaten up and crushed down for added effect. It's a real comedy out there.

And if he doesnt fire me, I will be a totally buckbroken employee who will never care about a rasie and work 60 hours weeks. Like a absurd housewife that has no were else to go and she tries to esapce and gets rapped(applies to ghost jobs) even worse so she comes back to him. Thats me. I used to think my relationship with work was like between a man and a woman, were I am the man that picks and chooses women and is the one who leaves but its all turned around past couple of years. I am nothing but a side pieace female whore to these companies now, they treat me like a contract worker without paying me like one. They got us with not giving raises to match inflation, they got us with return to work mandates and now, they are going to get us by firing us and leaving us with nothing. U will get put on a PIP too, at the worst time possible also. They wont even lay u off anymore, they want to save a little bit of money by denying us unemployment insurance by firing us through a PIP.


Was being in ur 30s a good excperince back in the day? There is no way human civilization would have gotten to were it is if it was always this bad for everyone. Of course, I am a faggot and just suck but the fsct of the matter is that PIPs and no jobs has been a very common trope on the internet lately
